title: Grigmin
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Grigmin |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blond]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Grigmin, I've warned you before. You put too much strain on your hardware. You could stay well within performance tolerances and still complete every maneuver in your routines. But instead you showboat, with junk heaps that were obsolete when the Clone Wars were news."
"Save the excuses, Solo. Will my airspeeder be ready for my afternoon show, or have you and your Wookiee sidekick decided you don't like working for me?|Han Solo and Grigmin|}} '''Grigmin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] stunt [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who operated [[Grigmin's Traveling Airshow]], a traveling barnstorming company, during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Grigmin was regarded as highly arrogant by many of his associates and employees, including the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who worked for Grigmin as pit crew for a brief period in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. The two clashed frequently, and Solo ultimately quit his job by upstaging Grigmin during a performance on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Saheelindeel]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|I trust Solo-my-friend, but not Grigmin-the-blowhard.|Fadoop|}} A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Grigmin earned fame during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] as a talented stunt [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], a career he pursued after winning a heavyweight championship in unarmed combat. After his fighting days ended, Grigmin took his flying skills on the road by operating a barnstorming outfit called [[Grigmin's Traveling Airshow]]. Grigmin primarily plied his trade on backwater [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], dazzling natives with tricks on a variety of outmoded craft, including a clunky, old [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]], a [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] and an [[X-222 fighter|X-222 high-altitude fighter]] for his grand finale. In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Grigmin picked up a pair of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], as his pit crew. Grigmin and Solo clashed frequently over Grigmin's arrogance and overtaxing of his [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]], concerns Grigmin constantly ignored. Things came to a head with Solo during a trip to the backwater world of [[Saheelindeel]], where Grigmin's tricks attracted a large crowd of the indigenous [[Saheelindeeli]]. Between repairs to Grigmin's airspeeder, Solo received a lucrative job offer to deliver learning materials to a [[University/Legends|university]] on [[Brigia/Legends|Brigia]], giving him the opportunity he had been waiting for to leave Grigmin behind. Wanting to take a shot at Grigmin on the way out, Solo borrowed an [[Fadoop|acquaintance's]] cobbled-together [[Skybarge|barge]] and upstaged him on his grand finale, infuriating Grigmin with his display of superior flying abilities. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I never trust these useless locals; it's a rule I have.|Grigmin|}} Tall, broad-shouldered and handsome, Grigmin was regarded by many around him, especially Han Solo, as an infuriatingly arrogant man who had a much greater assessment of his flying skills than was merited. In addition, many of his employees and those who had dealings with him regarded him as a deadbeat—although he paid, he did not always do so regularly. Grigmin felt himself far above the locals he entertained with his stunt flying, backing up his highhanded attitude with a confidence coming from the knowledge that due to his unarmed combat expertise, few would dare to take a swing. ==Behind the scenes== Grigmin first appeared in ''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1980]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{CSWECite|I|370|Grigmin}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Grigmin }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]]
